name: Suances to Santillana del Mar – circular via the Besaya Greenway and the coast
name_original: Suances a Santillana del Mar – circular por la Vía Verde del Besaya y la costa
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Suances offer diverse terrain along the Cantabrian Sea in northern Spain. The region features a rugged coastline with impressive cliffs and numerous beaches, alongside tranquil estuaries and lush green hills. River routes often follow the Saja and Besaya rivers, providing mostly paved paths. The landscape transitions from coastal paths to inland areas with varying elevation. Best touring cycling routes around Suances The most popular touring cycling route is Suances to Santillana del Mar – circular via the Besaya Greenway and the coast, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines coastal views with sections along the Besaya Greenway. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Faro de Suances y la Playa de Los Locos to the Estatua de Francisco de Asís – round trip via the Vía Verde del Besaya, an easy 12.7 miles (20.5 km) path. This route offers scenic coastal views and follows the Vía Verde del Besaya. Local touring cyclists also love the Ría de San Martín de la Arena loop from Hinojedo, a 10.9 miles (17.5 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Touring cycling around Suances is defined by dramatic coastlines, lush green hills, and river routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal paths to moderate routes with elevation gain. The routes in Suances are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Suances's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Suances?
A: Suances offers nearly 20 touring cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic coastline to tranquil river paths and lush green hills.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Suances?
A: You can expect a varied terrain in Suances. Routes often feature a rugged coastline with impressive cliffs and beautiful beaches, tranquil estuaries, and lush green hills. Many river routes, such as those along the Saja and Besaya rivers, offer mostly paved paths, while coastal paths might include some unpaved sections.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Suances?
A: Yes, Suances has several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. One excellent option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018654946/el-faro-de-suances-y-la-playa-de-los-locos-to-the-estatua-de-francisco-de-asis-round-trip-via-the-via-verde-del-besaya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Faro de Suances y la Playa de Los Locos to the Estatua de Francisco de Asís – round trip via the Vía Verde del Besaya&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 12.7-mile (20.5 km) path offering scenic coastal views and following the Vía Verde del Besaya.

Q: What are some popular moderate touring cycling routes in the Suances area?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31529455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de San Martín de la Arena loop from Hinojedo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice. This 10.9-mile (17.5 km) trail leads through picturesque riverine landscapes and can be completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40643510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of Francis of Assisi – Main Street of Riocorvo loop from Suances&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.8 km route with moderate elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Suances?
A: Absolutely! Many touring cycling routes in Suances are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012455969/suances-to-santillana-del-mar-circular-via-the-besaya-greenway-and-the-coast" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suances to Santillana del Mar – circular via the Besaya Greenway and the coast&lt;/a&gt;, a 20.2-mile (32.5 km) route that combines coastal views with sections along the Besaya Greenway.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Touring cycling routes in Suances offer stunning natural features like dramatic coastlines, impressive cliffs, and beautiful beaches such as Los Locos Beach and La Concha Beach. You can also explore tranquil estuaries and follow river banks. Nearby landmarks include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar Historic Town Centre&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/962386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Santa Juliana&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/640142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Costa Quebrada Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river paths?
A: Yes, Suances is characterized by lush green hills and the convergence of the Saja and Besaya rivers. Many cycling routes follow their banks, offering mostly paved surfaces and a blend of natural scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38269132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de la Concha (Suances) – Saja River Cycle Path loop from Suances&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, taking you along the Saja River.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Suances?
A: The touring cycling routes in Suances are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's dramatic coastlines, lush green hills, and the variety of routes available for different ability levels.

Q: Is Suances a good base for longer cycling holidays connecting to other towns?
A: Yes, Suances' proximity to other notable towns like Santillana del Mar and Comillas makes it an ideal base for longer cycling holidays. Routes often connect these destinations, allowing for extended exploration of the Cantabrian coast and inland areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Suances?
A: Suances, located on the Cantabrian Sea, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling from spring through autumn. The summer months provide warm weather, perfect for enjoying the beaches and coastal routes. However, the mild climate means that touring cycling is enjoyable for much of the year, with lush green landscapes to appreciate.

Q: Are there bike rental services available in Suances?
A: Yes, Suances caters well to touring cyclists with established services. Several local services offer bike rentals, including children's bikes and accessories like child seats and trailers, making it accessible for families to explore the area.
tips: An old laundry near the collegiate church of Santa Juliana in Santillana del Mar.

It is located in the historic center of the town of Santillana del Mar. The palace was completed in the mid-16th century for Alonso de Velarde. Its architecture is from the early Renaissance. In July 2018, the news broke that the building had been bought by a Mexican businessman who will invest in its recovery to turn the palace into a hotel.

The centuries-old core of the old town with its numerous small palaces and trading houses is ideal for a short sightseeing tour. It is particularly relaxed that no cars are allowed in the entire old town.
Ubiarco is a port marked by the greenery of the mountains and the blue of the waters, in the middle of a valley next to the sea.

Also known as Ubiarco Beach. Nearby there is an old hermitage located in a cavity formed in the core of a spectacular anticline fold. It is a small, clean beach, with some picnic tables with a wonderful view. Recommended to go at any time of the year.
El Sable Beach, located in Tagle, a town in the municipality of Suances (Cantabria), is a natural gem perfect for those seeking tranquility and scenic beauty.
The lighthouse is a few meters from the viewpoint of Playa de Los Locos. It started operating in 1863, serving as a reference for the ships in the port of Suances. There are magnificent views!
